examples.title: Generic Evolutionary Iteration Pseudocode
examples.description: Shows the basic structure of an evolutionary development process with iterations and feedback
examples.code:
// Evolutionary Process Pseudocode
BEGIN
 SET iteration_count = 0
 SET current_version = basic_system
 
 WHILE NOT complete AND iteration_count < max_iterations DO
 iteration_count = iteration_count + 1
 
 // Planning Phase
 PLAN next_requirements(current_version)
 
 // Risk Analysis
 IDENTIFY risks
 IF high_risks_exist THEN
 DEVELOP mitigation_strategies
 END IF
 
 // Engineering/Development
 BUILD incremental_changes
 INTEGRATE with current_version
 current_version = enhanced_system
 
 // Evaluation
 PRESENT to_customer
 GET feedback
 
 IF customer_satisfied THEN
 complete = TRUE
 ELSE
 ADJUST requirements based on feedback
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 FINALIZE system
 DELIVER product
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the cyclical nature of evolutionary development where each iteration plans, develops, evaluates, and refines based on feedback until the product meets customer requirements.

examples.title: Spiral Model Iteration Structure
examples.description: Pseudocode showing one iteration of the Spiral Model with all four phases
examples.code:
// Spiral Model - Single Iteration
BEGIN
 // Phase 1: Planning
 DETERMINE objectives for this iteration
 IDENTIFY constraints and resources
 PLAN activities and schedule
 
 // Phase 2: Risk Analysis
 IDENTIFY project risks (technical, schedule, budget)
 ANALYZE likelihood and impact of each risk
 IF risk > threshold THEN
 DEVELOP mitigation plan
 IMPLEMENT risk reduction measures
 END IF
 
 // Phase 3: Engineering
 IF risks_mitigated THEN
 DEVELOP prototype or product increment
 PERFORM coding and unit testing
 INTEGRATE with existing components
 ELSE
 ABORT or ADJUST project scope
 END IF
 
 // Phase 4: Evaluation
 EVALUATE deliverables with customer
 GET customer feedback
 PLAN next iteration based on feedback
 
 RETURN next_iteration_plan
END
examples.explanation: Each spiral iteration follows these four phases systematically. The iteration repeats until the product is complete and accepted. Risk analysis is the key differentiator from other evolutionary models.

examples.title: Prototyping Process Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm demonstrating the prototyping approach for requirement gathering
examples.code:
// Prototyping Process
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Initial Requirements
 GATHER initial requirements
 DESIGN system overview
 
 // Step 2: Quick Design (Prototype Framework)
 CREATE prototype_framework
 IDENTIFY core features to demonstrate
 
 // Step 3: Build Prototype
 BUILD working model (may use mockups, stubs)
 prototype = preliminary_version
 
 // Step 4: Customer Evaluation
 PRESENT prototype to customer
 GET evaluation feedback
 
 // Step 5: Refine Requirements
 WHILE prototype_needs_improvement DO
 IDENTIFY deficiencies from feedback
 MODIFY requirements
 ENHANCE prototype
 REPEAT evaluation
 END WHILE
 
 // Step 6: Final System Development
 IF prototype_approved THEN
 REPLACE prototype with production-quality code
 ADD missing features
 IMPROVE performance and reliability
 COMPLETE full system
 END IF
 
 DELIVER final system
END
examples.explanation: The prototyping process helps validate requirements before full development. The prototype serves as a communication tool between developers and stakeholders to ensure mutual understanding.
examples.spaceComplexity: Prototype: Small, Full system: Depends on requirements
